title: Boat Rental in Palau: Costs, Seasons, and Tips for 2026
html:
Boat rental in Palau ranges from €80 for a shared tour to over €2,000 per day for a crewed motor yacht, with departures from Malakal Harbor. The Rock Islands Southern Lagoon, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is the main cruising ground, and BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators and private owners.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Palau?

Renting a boat in Palau costs between €80 per person for a shared tour and €2,500 per day for a private crewed yacht. Prices vary by season, boat type, and duration. The high season from November to April sees the highest rates, while May to October offers discounts of 20, 30%.

  • Shared island-hopping tour: €80, €150 per person for a full day
  • Small motorboat (half-day, up to 6 guests): €350, €600
  • Catamaran (full-day, up to 12 guests): €900, €1,500
  • Crewed motor yacht (full-day, up to 8 guests): €1,200, €2,500

Where to set off in Palau

The main departure point is Malakal Harbor in Koror, the commercial and tourism hub. Other real marinas and anchorages include Sam's Dock, the Palau Royal Resort Marina, and the dock at Ngerulmud, the capital on Babeldaob. You can also set off from Peleliu Island or the quiet lagoon of Ulong, known for its diving and jellyfish.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small powerboat, Best for flexible half-day trips, up to 6 guests, from €350
  2. Sailing catamaran, Ideal for day charters with comfort, up to 12 guests, from €900
  3. Crewed motor yacht, For full-day luxury including captain and crew, up to 8 guests, from €1,200
  4. Rigid inflatable (RIB), Fast for island hopping, up to 6 guests, from €500 half-day

When choosing a boat, consider a crewed option if you want local knowledge. For example, the Mefasa 90 for hire is a 90-foot luxury motor yacht that includes a skipper and can accommodate up to eight guests. A guide to local anchorages is available on each boat listing.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Palau?

Palau does not require a boating licence for bareboat charter, but the operator sets its own competency checks. You may need to demonstrate basic handling skills or provide proof of experience. The boat owner confirms exact requirements before booking. For a hassle‑free trip, many visitors choose a crewed charter, especially for overnight passages.

Try a sunrise departure from Malakal Harbor, the Rock Islands are calm and uncrowded before 9 am, and you might spot dugongs near the channel.

Season and the best months for boating

The high season for boating in Palau runs from November to April, when the trade winds bring steady breezes and dry weather. The prevailing wind is the northeast monsoon, which makes for comfortable sailing and clear skies.

May to October is the wet season, with higher humidity and occasional squalls, but you can still enjoy the outer reefs and lower prices. To compare verified boat listings, you can hire a boat in Palau through BoatHire24 with transparent pricing and local advice.

At a glance

  • Price range (per day): €80 shared tour, €2,500 crewed yacht
  • Peak months: November to April
  • Typical trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Maximum guests on a private charter: 12
  • Local currency accepted: US dollar (USD), but quoted in EUR here

Practical advice for booking

Booking a boat in Palau is straightforward: you select a listing on BoatHire24, contact the owner directly via the platform, and pay a deposit to secure the date. Full details on how boat rental works are available in the support section. Always confirm whether fuel, food, and snorkel gear are included in the price.

Questions about boat rental in Palau

Can I rent a boat in Palau without a captain?

Yes, you can rent a bareboat if you hold a recognised powerboat licence or can demonstrate experience, but most operators prefer you take a local captain for safety and navigation through the reefs.

How far is it from Malakal Harbor to the Rock Islands?

It is about 10 nautical miles from Malakal Harbor to the main Rock Island lagoon, a 30‑minute boat ride in a RIB or speedboat.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Palau?

The best time is between November and April, when the weather is dry and the sea is calm, though May to October offers lower prices and fewer crowds.

Is Palau expensive for boat rental?

Boat rental in Palau is moderate for the Pacific; a shared tour costs from €80, while private charters range from €350 to €2,500 per day, depending on the vessel and season.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Palau or list your own. If you own a boat in Palau, you can rent out your boat and start earning from your asset in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Palau and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
